event.summary: Reviewed the bounded proposal and made it claimable without expanding its repositories, paths, or prerequisites.

acceptance.proof: The projection borrows only accepted Q state, validates the observation-bound structural domain, computes exact output bytes with checked arithmetic, enforces the caller limit, and returns one owned allocation without changing the source.
All seven accepted H cases execute through accepted local components and match the committed normalized public XML byte-for-byte, including declaration, LF, tab depth, desc spelling, and final LF.
LSLC-001G source, fixture, and receipt remain unchanged; compact explicit-tag serialization and observed document-envelope spelling remain separate public types and policies.
A childless description container other than the fixed desc root returns a typed indexed error instead of silently generalizing the observed empty-desc spelling.

The result is a local observation-bound string projection and claims no parser, canonical XML, endpoint or wire bytes, provider values, acquisition, clock/host/identity behavior, address/port semantics, networking, discovery, transport, runtime, device, adapter, feature, or Manifold authority.

Cargo and project locks, dependency and feature closure, accepted evidence, oracle drivers, runtime effects, devices, instructions, and public boundary remain unchanged or explicitly synchronized; portable workflow contracts pass.

document.commit_policy: Commit the observation-bound document-envelope projection separately from LSLC-001G, parsing, endpoint/wire compatibility, providers, acquisition, protocol, transport, adapters, runtime activation, features, devices, or external integration.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the observation-bound document envelope, exact spelling policy, G separation, fail-closed unobserved shape, and provider/runtime boundary.
Describe the bounded observed envelope without promoting it to endpoint, wire, provider, or runtime compatibility.
Review whether observation-bound representation separation adds reusable guidance; retain unchanged if current artifact-role and evidence rules cover it.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review whether the local LSL document-envelope boundary changes repo-family routing; retain unchanged if current public and authority boundaries cover it.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

non_scope: Any modification to LSLC-001G source or accepted evidence, Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock, project or feature locks, other accepted fixtures, oracle drivers, dependencies, Cargo features, unsafe Rust, native libraries, generated protocol material, or implementation-source inputs.

XML parsing, decoding, canonicalization, arbitrary declaration policy, attributes, namespaces, schemas, mixed content, comments, processing instructions beyond the fixed observed declaration, CRLF output, or general-purpose pretty printing.

Endpoint or wire compatibility claims, provider traits or selection, clocks, host/environment inspection, identity generation, address/port interpretation or ownership, sockets, networking, discovery, inlet, outlet, protocol, transport, buffering, recovery, FFI, adapters, or runtime activation.
Device, Quest, Hostess, platform lifecycle, package, permission, product-policy, external-integration, or ecosystem compatibility claims.
Manifold admission, registry, subscription, routing, lease, revision, replay, identity, authorization, audit, command, session, topology, transport, provider epoch, or product-policy authority.
document.objective: Borrow one accepted LSLC-001Q ordered info element tree and project it into one explicitly byte-bounded owned UTF-8 string using only the LSLC-001H-observed XML declaration, LF newline, tab indentation, final newline, and empty desc spelling, while leaving LSLC-001G unchanged and adding no parser, endpoint, wire, provider, acquisition, transport, runtime, device, feature, or Manifold authority behavior.
outputs: A dependency-free borrowed projection from accepted StreamInfoOrderedXml to one owned bounded UTF-8 document-envelope string without modifying the source tree or LSLC-001G.
Exact fixed policy for the observed XML 1.0 declaration, LF line endings, one tab per element depth below info, observed static/volatile/desc order, final LF, and desc-only empty-element spelling.
Checked output-length arithmetic, limit rejection before exact output allocation, one exact fallible traversal-frame reserve, and typed errors retaining node indexes and requested or required counts.
Seven-case evidence matching accepted normalized LSLC-001H public XML bytes and a fail-closed rejection for childless non-desc containers outside the observed structural domain.
